problem.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array II
problem.description: Given the array nums after the possible rotation and an integer target, return true if target is in nums, or false if it is not in nums. The array may contain duplicates.
assumptions: Array may contain duplicates (harder than version I).
Array was originally sorted and then rotated at some pivot.

example.explanation: When nums[lo] == nums[mid] == nums[hi], we cannot determine which half is sorted. Safely increment lo and decrement hi. Otherwise apply the same sorted-half logic as version I.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Binary Search with Duplicate Handling
solutions.description: Extend the version-I binary search with a third case: when nums[lo] == nums[mid] == nums[hi], we cannot tell which half is sorted, so we shrink both ends by 1. In other cases, identify the sorted half normally and check if target falls within it. Worst case degrades to O(n) with all duplicates.
